_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 1-5 Shock (Revised 06/01/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan General Treatment Protocol Section 1-5: Shock — MDHHS Approved 6/1/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: Shock
points: Assessment: consider etiologies of shock and refer to specific types first if known — Anaphylaxis/Allergic Reaction-Treatment Protocol, Hemorrhagic Shock-Treatment Protocol, Pulmonary Edema/Cardiogenic Shock-Treatment Protocol
Pediatric patients (< 14 years of age): utilize MI MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage; when unavailable, use pediatric dosing listed within protocol
Control major bleeding per Bleeding Control (BCON)-Procedure Protocol
Remove all transdermal patches using gloves
Prompt transport per MCA Transport Protocol
Special consideration: if 3rd trimester pregnancy, position patient left lateral recumbent
Obtain vascular access in a manner that will not delay transport (Specialist skill)

Administer NS or LR IV/IO fluid bolus (Specialist skill — refer to Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol); Adults: up to 1 liter wide open; Pediatrics: up to 20 mL/kg based on signs and symptoms of shock; slow to TKO when SBP > 90 mmHg
Consider establishing a second large bore IV of NS or LR enroute to the hospital (Specialist skill)
Obtain 12-lead ECG if suspected cardiac etiology (per MCA selection — may be BLS or Specialist procedure) per 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol
If accompanying head injury, refer to Head Injury-Treatment Protocol: maintain SpO2 ≥ 90%; maintain SBP > 90 mmHg and < 140 mmHg; do NOT hyperventilate

Hypotensive patients should receive additional IV/IO fluid boluses as indicated (consider preparing epi push dose while administering second bolus): Adults repeat to maximum 2 liters; Pediatrics repeat 20 mL/kg to maximum 40 mL/kg; monitor for pulmonary edema — if presents, stop fluids and contact Medical Control

If hypotension persists after IV/IO fluid bolus: administer epinephrine IV/IO by push dose (dilute boluses) while administering second fluid bolus (Specialist skill — prepare epinephrine 10 mcg/mL by combining 1 mL of 1 mg/10 mL epinephrine in 9 mL NS); Adults: 10–20 mcg (1–2 mL of epi 10 mcg/mL) IV/IO, repeat q3–5 min, titrate SBP > 90 mmHg; Pediatrics: 1 mcg/kg (0.1 mL of epi 10 mcg/mL) IV/IO, maximum dose 10 mcg (1 mL), repeat q3–5 min

mca_selections_required.description: May be designated as a BLS skill or Specialist (AEMT/Paramedic) skill

key_drugs.name: NS or LR IV/IO Fluid Bolus
key_drugs.dose: Adults: up to 1 liter (repeat to max 2 L); Pediatrics: up to 20 mL/kg (repeat to max 40 mL/kg)
key_drugs.indication: Shock — hypotension, signs of poor perfusion
key_drugs.contraindications: Suspected cardiogenic shock or pulmonary edema — slow to TKO; refer to Pulmonary Edema/Cardiogenic Shock protocol
key_drugs.notes: Slow to TKO when SBP > 90 mmHg. Monitor for pulmonary edema. Contact Medical Control if pulmonary edema develops.

key_drugs.name: Epinephrine (Push Dose) IV/IO
key_drugs.dose: Prepare 10 mcg/mL: combine 1 mL of 1 mg/10 mL epi in 9 mL NS; Adults: 10–20 mcg (1–2 mL) IV/IO q3–5 min, titrate SBP > 90 mmHg; Pediatrics: 1 mcg/kg (0.1 mL), max 10 mcg (1 mL), repeat q3–5 min
key_drugs.indication: Hypotension persisting after IV/IO fluid bolus
key_drugs.contraindications: Use only after fluid bolus attempted; not for hypertensive patients
key_drugs.notes: Administer while administering second fluid bolus. Titrate to SBP > 90 mmHg.

deterioration_flags: Hypotension not responding to initial fluid bolus — consider push-dose epinephrine (Specialist skill); reassess for underlying etiology
Signs of pulmonary edema during fluid resuscitation — stop fluids immediately; contact Medical Control
Head injury with shock — maintain SBP > 90 mmHg AND < 140 mmHg; do NOT hyperventilate; refer to Head Injury protocol
Bleeding source identified — control with BCON protocol before fluid resuscitation
Suspected cardiac etiology — obtain 12-lead ECG; refer to appropriate Cardiac Protocol
scope_notes: 12-Lead ECG: scope per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
Cardiac monitoring: Specialist skill unless MCA-approved otherwise
